footnote: Titles count the whole French women's championship back to 1974–75 — the group-stage era included; the title has been decided by play-offs since 2024. Honours follow club continuity: Paris FC carry Juvisy's six titles, while FC Lyon's four stay with FC Lyon despite the 2004 section transfer to OL. The 12 clubs shown are the 2025–26 season's until debutants Saint-Malo kick off in the autumn.

teams.name: Dijon FCO
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 1–0 to Montpellier at the Poussots in their 2018 debut; never relegated since, with a fourth place in 2025 that put them in the league's first play-offs.

teams.name: FC Fleury 91
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 5–1 to Paris FC in their 2017 debut after taking over Val d'Orge's promotion; a town of 9,500 — best known for housing Europe's largest prison — has faced Lyon and PSG every season since.

teams.name: Le Havre AC
teams.firstGameNote: France's oldest club won its 2020 top-flight debut 4–0 at Issy — then took eight more points all season and went straight down; back and established since 2022.

teams.name: RC Lens
teams.firstGameNote: Thrashed 6–0 at Juvisy in the lineage's 2012 debut as Arras FCF, the club Lens absorbed in 2020; the Lens-branded elite debut in 2025 drew a record 10,712 to Bollaert — and ended in relegation anyway.

teams.name: Olympique de Marseille
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 1–1 at Bordeaux in the modern section's 2016 debut; OM's original women's team played the 1970s group-era top flight, reaching the 1979 semi-finals before dissolving.

lastTitle.clinch: won 9–0 at Condé-sur-Noireau in their final match, putting the title beyond Juvisy's reach a day before their rival's finale

teams.name: FC Nantes
teams.firstGameNote: Won their 2024 top-flight debut 1–0 at Le Havre on a 35-metre lob — a birthday present for their 30-year-old coach — a decade after starting in the third district division.

teams.name: OL Lyonnes

lastTitle.clinch: crushed Paris FC 5–0 in the play-off final at home — two for Chawinga, a Dumornay hat-trick — for title number 19 and a fifth straight

teams.lineage: The women's section came over from FC Lyon in 2004; FC Lyon's four 1990s titles stay with that club. Rebranded OL Lyonnes in 2025.

teams.name: Paris FC

lastTitle.clinch: crowned as FCF Juvisy without playing, when Montpellier's defeat at Soyaux settled the race with two rounds to spare

teams.lineage: The section is the former FCF Juvisy, integrated into Paris FC in 2017 with its six titles.

lastTitle.clinch: beat Dijon 3–0 at Jean-Bouin on the final day to finish a point ahead of Lyon, unbeaten — the only interruption to two decades of Lyon

teams.name: AS Saint-Étienne
teams.firstGameNote: Beat Soyaux 1–0 in the lineage's verifiable 2007 debut as Racing Club de Saint-Étienne — a club founded the day after ASSE shut its women's section in 1977, folded back in by 2009; relegated again in 2026.

teams.name: RC Strasbourg
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 1–1 with Dijon at the Meinau in the club's first-ever top-flight match in 2024 — regional football to the Première Ligue in eight years — then leapt to seventh in season two.
